origin.name: Portugal
origin.post_mortem_trigger: Sudden, violent, suspicious, or unexplained deaths
origin.multilingual_note: A multilingual death certificate is available under EU convention and may be accepted directly without separate translation.
origin.algarve_note: Deaths in the Algarve are the most common. Local funeral directors are highly experienced with British repatriation requirements.
origin.source: FCDO Portugal travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Lisbon guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Certidao de obito (multilingual version if available)

airlines.name: TAP Air Portugal
airlines.routes: LIS, OPO to LHR, LGW, MAN

airlines.name: easyJet
airlines.routes: FAO, LIS, OPO to UK regional airports

special_rules: Ministerio Publico orders a post-mortem for sudden, violent, suspicious, or unexplained deaths. Adds 1-4 weeks.
Algarve funeral directors are experienced with British repatriations. Faro airport (FAO) has direct UK connections.
Multilingual death certificate under EU convention may remove need for separate certified translation.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. A multilingual EU death certificate from Portugal may be accepted directly. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Lisbon can register the death and advise. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Portugal to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Repatriation from Portugal to the UK takes 7-14 days. Algarve and Lisbon cases handled. All documentation arranged. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland
key_documents: Certidao de obito (multilingual version if available)

airlines.name: Ryanair
airlines.routes: FAO, LIS, OPO to DUB, ORK, NOC

special_rules: Ferry from Lisbon to Cork is not available. All repatriations are by air cargo.
Irish Embassy in Lisbon handles consular matters.
Multilingual death certificate may remove the need for a separate certified English translation.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. A multilingual EU death certificate may be accepted directly. The Coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. The Irish Embassy in Lisbon can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Portugal to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Portugal and needs to come home to Ireland. Repatriation takes 7-14 days. All documentation handled. Contact us 24/7.
